name: libp2p
what_it_does: Modular peer-to-peer networking library used by IPFS, Ethereum, Polkadot, and Filecoin for peer discovery, NAT traversal, multiplexed transport, and encrypted connections.
use_cases: Build decentralized applications requiring peer discovery and direct peer-to-peer communication without a central server
Implement gossip protocols and pub/sub messaging across a P2P network of nodes
Add NAT traversal and hole punching to enable direct connections between peers behind firewalls
Embed encrypted multiplexed transport (QUIC, WebSocket, TCP) into a custom distributed system
not_for: Teams needing a REST API or managed service; libp2p is a library requiring significant integration work
Use cases where a traditional client-server architecture or message broker is more appropriate
best_when: Best when building decentralized or distributed systems where peers communicate directly and there is no central server to coordinate connections.
avoid_when: Avoid when your use case can be served by a message broker or REST API; libp2p's complexity is only justified for genuine P2P architectures.

auth.notes: All connections are encrypted by default using the Noise protocol or TLS 1.3. Peer identity is based on cryptographic keypairs (Ed25519, RSA, secp256k1). No traditional auth methods.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free; MIT and Apache 2.0 licensed implementations
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; Protocol Labs provides community support
pricing.notes: Completely free and open-source. Infrastructure costs are self-managed. Protocol Labs maintains the reference implementations.

performance.rate_limits: No platform-imposed rate limits; performance depends entirely on network topology and implementation

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Library-level errors are language-idiomatic rather than standardized; error handling patterns vary by implementation language.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Connection establishment is not idempotent; peer discovery and pub/sub subscriptions may produce duplicate events without careful deduplication.
known_agent_gotchas: No REST API; agents must embed the library and operate at the Go/JS/Rust level, which is not compatible with typical HTTP-based agent tool patterns
Peer discovery is probabilistic and non-deterministic; agents cannot guarantee finding a specific peer without a known bootstrap node
NAT traversal requires relay nodes (circuit relays) to be available; direct connectivity is not guaranteed in all network environments
Each language implementation has different APIs and feature coverage; documentation is fragmented across implementations

security_score_components.security_notes: Cryptographic identity using Ed25519 keypairs and Noise protocol encryption by default is a strong security foundation. No traditional auth scopes; access is all-or-nothing per peer connection.
